zh.Meisgyn,Penychen,Glamorganshire,Wales: 英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根
zh.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris是Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris的女兒，於大約1283年出生在威爾士。她和Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham至少有1個兒子。她去世在宏都拉斯巴耶。

zh-hans.Meisgyn,Penychen,Glamorganshire,Wales: 英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris是Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris的女儿，于大约1283年出生在威尔士。她和Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham至少有1个儿子。她去世在洪都拉斯巴耶。

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: German: occupational name for a ferryman, Middle High German verje, verge.

nameMeanings.name: leuaf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English lef, leff, lief, life ‘beloved’ (Old English lēof ‘beloved; dear one, sweetheart’).

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English personal name Lef. This could represent either Old Norse Leifr (see Leif 1) or Old English Lēof which, though unrecorded, is a very probable short form of Old English names such as Lēofrīc, Lēofwine. Compare Leif 3.
Americanized form of Swedish Lööf, Löf, or Löv: ornamental name from löv ‘leaf’. Compare Leif 2.

variants.name: Leif
meanings: Swedish: from the Old Norse personal name Leifr, a short form of the compound names with the second element leifr (from leif ‘heir’).
Americanized form of Swedish Lööf, Löf, or Löv (see Leaf 3).
English: rare variant of   Leaf  .

variants.name: Levick
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English personal name Lefeke, Old English Lēofeca, a derivative of Lēofa (see Leaf ).
English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Anglo-Norman French l'eveske ‘the bishop’ (see Bishop ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Yiddish Leyvik, a pet form of the personal name Leyvi, itself a form of the Biblical name Levi (see Levy ).

variants.name: Leacock
meanings: English:
from the Middle English personal name Levecok, a pet form of Leve, see Leaf .
variant of   Laycock  .

variants.name: Love
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name derived from the Old English female name Lufu ‘love’, or the masculine equivalent Lufa. Compare Leaf 2.

English and Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French love ‘she-wolf’ or simply ‘wolf’, the word love being a back-formation from the diminutive forms lovel and lovet ‘little wolf’. See also Low 3, from Anglo-Norman French lou, the more usual form of the word.
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionghuin (see McKinnon ).

variants.name: Life
meanings: English: variant of   Leaf  .

variants.name: Liff
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yechiel, Chaim.
English: variant of   Leaf  .
Jewish: variant of   Lief  .

variants.name: Lief
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Varda, Meyer.
English: variant of   Leaf  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Livonia (now a part of Latvia), from German Liv.

variants.name: Leef
meanings: English: variant of   Leaf  .

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Turkish: ornamental name or nickname from ay ‘Moon’, by extension also ‘beautiful face’.
Hungarian (Áy): unexplained.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 艾, see   Ai   2.

variants.name: Ai
meanings: Japanese: usually written 阿井 with characters used phonetically. The meaning could be ‘meeting’. In North America, it may also be a shortened form of some other name.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 艾, referring to ‘Chinese mugwort’, a type of plant used in traditional Chinese medical treatment: (i) perhaps from the second element of Ru Ai (汝艾), the name of an official who lived during the reign of Shao Kang, the 6th king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ai Ling (艾陵), the name of a hill (located in Shandong province), the fief of Ai Kong (艾孔), an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) from the placename Ai (艾), the name of a fief (located in Jiangxi province) which was granted to an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) adopted as a surname by the Qu Jin (去斤), Qu Ai (去艾), and Ai Jin (艾斤) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (v) from Ai (艾), the name of an ancient state (possibly in present-day Jiangxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 愛, meaning ‘love’ in Chinese: (i) said to be traced back to Ai Xie Wu (愛邪勿), prime minister of the state of Huihu (known as Uyghur Empire or Uyghur Khaganate), who was endowed with the surname Ai (愛) by Emperor Wuzong of Tang (814–846  AD ). (ii) a Han Chinese surname for some members of the Aisin Gioro family from the Manchu ethnic group, known as the royal family of the Qing dynasty (1616 – 1911  AD ). (iii) a surname from minority ethnic groups in Taiwan.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Madog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French Canadian: variant of   Madore  .

variants.name: Madore
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Eusebe, Fernand, Lucien, Normand, Adhemar, Adrien, Donat, Elzear, Francois, Gaetan, Gilles.
French Canadian: from a French shortened form of the Spanish personal name Amador, from Latin Amator (from amator ‘lover’), a Christian saint's name. Compare Mador .
In some cases possibly also Breton (Madoré): nickname meaning literally ‘good help’, from Old Breton mat ‘good’ and uuoret ‘help’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

en.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris was born about 1283, in Wales as the daughter of Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris. She had at least 1 son with Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham. She died in Valle, Honduras.

fr.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris est née environ 1283, à Galles, fille de Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham. Elle est décédée à Valle, Honduras.

de.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris wurde um 1283 in Wales, als Tochter von Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris geboren. Sie hatte mit Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in Valle, Honduras.

ht.Meisgyn,Penychen,Glamorganshire,Wales: Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris è nata il circa 1283 nel Galles come figlia di Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham. Morì nel Valle, Honduras.

ja.Meisgyn,Penychen,Glamorganshire,Wales: イギリスウェールズグラモーガン
ja.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudrisは約1283年にウェールズでLeuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudrisの娘として生まれた。彼女はGruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellinghamとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女はホンジュラスヴァルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris(은)는 대략 1283년 웨일스에서 Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 온두라스 발에서 사망했다.

mg.Meisgyn,Penychen,Glamorganshire,Wales: Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

mn.Meisgyn,Penychen,Glamorganshire,Wales: Teyrnas Glywysing, Уэльс, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris nasceu aproximadamente 1283, em Gales filha de Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham. Ela faleceu em Valle, Honduras.

ru.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris родилась около 1283 г. в местности Уэльс. Её отца звали Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham. Она умерла в местности валл, Гондурас.

es.lifeSummary: Tangwystl ferch leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris nació aproximadamente 1283, en Gales como hija de Leuaf ab Mareddydd ap Madog of Bodiudris. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Gruffydd Llwyd ap Llywelyn Later made Lord of Gellingham. Murió en Valle, Honduras.
